full_text_rationale: Full text describes the Darwin Godel Machine maintaining an archive of generated coding agents, selecting archived agents for self-modification, preserving auditable lineages of code changes/evaluations, and improving on SWE-bench and Polyglot with baselines for self-improvement and archive/open-ended exploration.
titulo: Darwin Godel Machine: Open-Ended Evolution of Self-Improving Agents
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 004; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Jenny Zhang, Shengran Hu, Cong Lu, Robert Lange, Jeff Clune
formato_armazenamento: Growing archive/tree of generated coding-agent codebases, benchmark evaluations, and auditable lineages; archived agents can be selected as parents for self-modification.
llm_utilizado: Claude 3.5 Sonnet (New) for self-modification and SWE-bench evaluation; o3-mini for Polyglot evaluation; transfer experiments with Claude 3.7 are discussed.
metricas_desempenho: Solved-task percentage / pass@1; best-agent score over iterations; cost estimate; baseline comparison curves
baseline_comparado: DGM without self-improving agents, DGM without open-ended exploration, checked open-source SWE-bench SoTA, Aider on Polyglot
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Archive grows over 80 iterations, generating one new agent per iteration in the reported setup; all compilable agents are retained as potential stepping stones.
custo_por_tarefa: Estimated cost for one SWE-bench DGM run is about $22,000; each baseline SWE-bench run is about $10,000; one 60-task SWE-bench evaluation costs about $350 and one 60-task Polyglot evaluation about $5.
resultado_principal: After 80 iterations, DGM improves coding-agent performance from 20.0% to 50.0% on SWE-bench and from 14.0% to 38.0% on the Polyglot subset; full Polyglot improves from 14.2% to 30.7%.
desempenho_vs_baseline: DGM outperforms variants without self-improvement and without open-ended exploration; best discovered SWE-bench agent is comparable to checked open-source human-designed SoTA, while Polyglot improves 14.2% to 30.7% on the full benchmark.
custo_vs_baseline: DGM SWE-bench run is about $22,000 versus about $10,000 for either baseline run, so performance gains come at materially higher API cost.
limitacoes: Requires extensive compute and significant API cost; assumes coding benchmark gains reflect better self-improvement ability; archive maintenance/parent selection is fixed rather than self-modified; safety requires sandboxing, resource limits, and human oversight.
ameacas_validade: Uses staged subsets for cost control; results depend on Claude/o3-mini models, benchmark sampling, stochastic self-modification, and sandboxed execution constraints.
